title: QA: dangerously_disable_sandbox in tool args only logs, doesn't deny (permissions.rs)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/permissions.rs:155-169
## Violation
When the model tries to inject `dangerously_disable_sandbox: true` into a
Bash tool call, the permission manager only logs a warning and falls
through to normal rule processing:
```rust
if canonical_tool == "Bash" {
    if let Some(disable) = tool_args.get("dangerously_disable_sandbox") {
        if disable.as_bool().unwrap_or(false) {
            warn!(
                tool = %canonical_tool,
                target = %target,
                "Model attempted dangerously_disable_sandbox=true in tool args — IGNORED. \
                 This flag is only honored from user-level configuration."
            );
        }
    }
}
```
The flag is "ignored" — but the call still proceeds through the normal
checks. If `default_allow` matches the command, it runs. A clear prompt
injection attempt by the model results in zero defensive action beyond a
log line that may or may not be audited. The test
`test_dangerously_disable_sandbox_in_tool_args_is_ignored` confirms the
call still falls through to `NeedsPrompt` in the no-rules case but does
NOT test the `default_allow=["*"]` case.
## Mandated refactor
Return `CheckResult::Denied("Model attempted to disable sandbox via tool
args; refusing")` instead of warn-and-continue. A model attempting this
injection is hostile and should not get any further permission
evaluation. Add a counter / metric for "sandbox bypass attempts" so
operators can detect compromised sessions. Add a test that proves the
call is denied even with `default_allow=["**"]`.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
